title: Morning FX & Macro Review — 12 May 2026
headline: Dollar firmer into US CPI; FOMC minutes lean patient; PBoC fixes test the topside
executive_summary:
CPI is the binary. Everything else is conditioning.

Dollar bid against the funders overnight on a soft Asia equity tape and a clean break of 1.0890 in EUR/USD. Front-end Treasury yields nudged 3bp higher as the market trimmed July cut conviction; 2y now sits 22bp above the Dec-26 OIS rate, the widest gap since March. The Treasury 3y stop-through earlier this week did the heavy lifting; today's CPI is the next swing factor.

Base case: 3.1% headline / +0.3% core m/m. In-line print sells the front-end belly and lifts the dollar another 30-40 ticks against the funders. A soft surprise (≤+0.2% core) is the asymmetric tail — dollar can give back the week's gains in an hour. Hot surprise (+0.4%+ core) re-prices the September cut entirely.

Positioning is moderately USD-long going in; spec longs in DXY cut 11k in the past two weeks per CFTC. Risk-reversal skew has rebuilt JPY-call bid, suggesting the street is hedging a soft-CPI dollar reversal via USD/JPY topside. Stay nimble.
fx_commentary:
EUR/USD broke 1.0890 cleanly in Asia, with the 1.0860 pivot now the operative line. A clean break and hourly close below 1.0860 opens 1.0820 (50dma) and 1.0790 (200dma). On the topside, 1.0915 caps; only a daily close above 1.0945 reverses the bias.

USD/JPY drifted to 152.30 without topside vol cover — the 1m 25-delta risk reversal flipped JPY-call bid for the first time since the BoJ June meeting. We like buying convex topside via 1m 154 calls; spot carry funds the premium through 152.50.

GBP/USD heavy on the cross. EUR/GBP grinding back to 0.8540 as Gilt curve flattened post-Mansion House. Watch PM Starmer's fiscal language at the dispatch box at 12:00 LDN — any softening of the fiscal rule sells the front-end Gilt curve, which sells the pound.

AUD/USD pinned in 0.6580-0.6620 range; awaiting Q1 wage price index Wed for the next directional cue.
rates_commentary:
Front-end USD rates dragging the curve. 2y Treasury yields +3.2bp at 4.31%, the highest level since the April CPI miss. The market is pricing 38bp of cuts through year-end, vs 52bp two weeks ago. We see the front end as cheap into the data; soft CPI catches up 8-12bp.

EUR rates outperforming USD by 4bp on the day. Schatz yields -1.1bp at 2.34%; Bund flatter on the day. ECB pricing now shows 32bp of cuts through Dec-26, broadly in line with our base case.

The 5s30s curve flattened 1.5bp; we hold the steepener with a stop-through Friday's close.
equities_commentary:
Asia mixed: Nikkei -0.7% on the JPY-cross reversal, Hang Seng +0.4% on PBoC liquidity injection. European futures call flat, modest tilt to defensives. S&P futures -0.15% pre-data.

NVDA earnings tonight the second-tier catalyst — semis trading rich into the print; any miss extends the small-cap underperformance theme.
commodities_commentary:
Brent steady at $87.40 ahead of EIA inventories; backwardation intact at $1.20 1m-6m. Gold $2,945 holding 200dma support; net specs cut 4.2k contracts last week.

Copper +0.3% on China stimulus narrative; LME 3m at $9,820/t. We continue to see asymmetric upside on any incremental policy.

risk_themes: US CPI binary — base case 3.1% headline / +0.3% core
ECB Schnabel QT framing — hawkish risk

strategist_view.headline: CPI is the binary; everything else is conditioning
strategist_view.body: We enter today's session with the dollar moderately bid against the funders and front-end USD rates pricing 38bp of cuts through year-end. That's roughly 14bp less easing than was priced two weeks ago, with the front end now ~22bp above the Dec-26 OIS — the widest gap since March. The cleanest expression of a soft-CPI surprise is therefore receiving the 2y belly: ~12bp catch-up on a sub-+0.2% core print. The cleanest expression of an in-line print is the EUR/USD downside trade we've been carrying since 1.0925; 1.0820 is the operative target, with a stop on a daily close above 1.0945.

macro_overview.opening:
Dollar firmer into US CPI; FOMC minutes lean patient; PBoC fixes test the topside.

The overnight tape was a clean continuation of the post-NFP USD rebuild. Asia equities heavy, with Nikkei -0.7% on the JPY-cross reversal and Hang Seng staying bid on the PBoC liquidity injection. European futures call flat, with a modest defensive tilt — utilities and staples outperforming cyclicals by 30-40bp at the open.

The front-end USD curve did the heavy lifting overnight. 2y Treasury yields +3.2bp at 4.31% as the market trimmed July-cut conviction further. Money-market futures now imply 38bp of cuts through year-end vs 52bp two weeks ago — the most hawkish front-end pricing since April's CPI miss. We see the front end as cheap going in; today's CPI will swing it 8-12bp in either direction.
macro_overview.whats_moving:
Two distinct flows.

Real-money sellers in EUR/USD overnight after the 1.0890 break, with the bulk of the volume concentrated in the 1.0875-1.0890 zone. CTA models flipped short on the daily close below 1.0900 — that's another ~$3-4bn of EUR selling pressure pending. On the other side, leveraged accounts paying USD/JPY topside vol; 1m 25-delta risk reversal flipped JPY-call bid for the first time since the BoJ June meeting, with the street hedging a soft-CPI USD reversal via convex topside.
macro_overview.rates_view:
Front-end USD rates are the cleanest expression of the day. 2y at 4.31% sits 22bp above the Dec-26 OIS — historically rich on a 6m lookback. Soft CPI catches that up 8-12bp; in-line keeps it bid; hot extends another 5-7bp.

The 5s30s curve flattened 1.5bp on the session; we hold the steepener with a stop-through Friday's close. The trade is positively-carried, ~$1.2k per bp per million, and tracks the long-end auction concession that builds into the 10y supply at 17:00 GMT.

The FOMC minutes at 19:00 GMT are the second binary. Patient language is the consensus expectation (~60% probability) but a meaningful minority of participants flagged services-inflation persistence at the April meeting per the post-meeting press. Any reference to a higher-for-longer narrative re-prices the September cut.
macro_overview.cross_asset_thesis: USD-long is the consensus trade going in. That's both the strength of the setup and its risk — positioning is moderately heavy, and the soft-CPI reversal would be sharp. We're playing the dollar long via short EUR/USD spot (entered 1.0925, target 1.0820, stop above 1.0945) and convex USD/JPY topside (1m 154 calls funded by the carry). Equities are a second-order play — soft CPI extends the small-cap underperformance theme, hot CPI is a duration shock that hits the entire complex.

what_changed.summary: Front-end USD rates re-priced 14bp more hawkish vs two weeks ago; EUR/USD broke the 1.0890 pivot cleanly; JPY-call skew rebuilt for the first time since June.

key_takeaways.text: CPI is the binary — base case 3.1% headline / +0.3% core. Anything else re-prices the curve.
Front-end USD rates 22bp above Dec-26 OIS — cheap on a soft surprise.
EUR/USD 1.0890 broken; 1.0820 the next operative level.
FOMC minutes the secondary binary at 19:00 — patient language is consensus.
JPY-call skew rebuilt; street hedging soft-CPI USD reversal via topside.

trade_ideas.catalyst: US CPI 13:30 GMT + FOMC minutes 19:00 GMT
trade_ideas.rationale: Levered USD-long, real-money receiving belly. Path lower on soft print. Size 2/3 pre-data; cut on 1.0890 reclaim failure.
2y still 22bp above Dec-26 OIS. Soft CPI + stop-through auction = 8-12bp catch-up. Hedge tail via DXY topside calls.

trade_ideas.rationale: RR flipped JPY-call bid. Spot drifted without topside cover. Convex tail cheap; carry-positive through 152.50.

trade_ideas.catalyst: OPEC+ extension + EIA draw + headline risk
trade_ideas.rationale: Spec longs cut 11k in 2w. Backwardation intact ($1.20 1m-6m). Headline premium leaked — fair-to-cheap. Asymmetric on geo noise.
SNB tolerance for CHF strength tested at 0.96. ECB minutes lean patient. Carry +80bp ann.; hawkish-CPI safe-haven flow is the stop.

If CPI core ≤+0.2% → July cut prob to 65%, 2y −10-14bp
If minutes flag services-inflation concerns → curve flattens 4-6bp
Lagarde Wed Q&A — June cut confirmation tone

Wage data Q1 final read Wed

pair_commentary.note: Clean break of 1.0890 in Asia. CTA models flipped short on close < 1.0900. Operative line is 1.0860; clean h-close below opens 1.0820. Topside capped at 1.0915. Pre-positioned short.
RR flipped JPY-call bid for first time since BoJ June. Convex topside cheap; we are long 1m 154 strike calls. Spot carry funds the premium through 152.50.
Heavy on the cross. EUR/GBP grinding back to 0.8540. Starmer's fiscal language at PMQs is the variable — softening sells front-end Gilt, sells cable.
Range-bound. Waiting on Q1 wage price index Wed. Risk-off bias on hot US CPI.
Driven by EUR/CHF dynamic. We are short EUR/CHF separately.

session_breakdown.asia: Tape was a clean continuation of post-NFP USD rebuild. Nikkei -0.7% on JPY-cross reversal as USD/JPY ground to 152.30. Hang Seng +0.4% on PBoC liquidity injection (¥80bn 7d RR). PBoC fix at 7.1842 — back to defending topside. AUD/USD pinned in 0.6580-0.6620 range awaiting Q1 wages Wed.
session_breakdown.europe: European futures call flat with defensive tilt — utilities and staples outperforming cyclicals by 30-40bp at the open. Schatz -1.1bp at 2.34%; Bund flatter on the session. ZEW expectations at 09:00 GMT; ECB Schnabel at 10:00 GMT the variable. PM Starmer PMQs at 12:00 LDN with GBP cross-asset implications.
session_breakdown.us: Pre-CPI quiet — S&P futures -0.15%, 10y +1.2bp at 4.45%. CPI at 13:30 the primary catalyst; 10y auction at 17:00; FOMC minutes at 19:00. Williams at 19:00 the first post-CPI Fed voice. NVDA earnings tonight a second-order catalyst — semis trading rich into the print.

cross_asset.title: Rates → FX
cross_asset.body: Front-end USD rates are the cleanest driver. 2y at 4.31% sits 22bp above Dec-26 OIS — the widest gap since March. The cleanest expression of a soft-CPI surprise is therefore receiving the 2y belly + EUR/USD recovery.

cross_asset.title: FX → Equities
cross_asset.body: JPY-cross reversal feeding Japan equity weakness. Nikkei -0.7% on the back of USD/JPY topside. For US equities, the dollar is a second-order driver — primary remains rates.

cross_asset.title: Commodities → FX
cross_asset.body: Brent backwardation intact at $1.20 1m-6m supporting CAD on the cross. USD/CAD 1.3722 the level to watch — any clean break of 1.3680 with sustained Brent strength opens 1.3580.

cross_asset.title: Rates → Equities
cross_asset.body: S&P 500 fwd P/E at 19.4x vs 10y at 4.45% gives an ERP of ~2.7%, in the bottom quintile of the past decade. Any further front-end re-pricing higher tests the duration tolerance of the index.

risk_warnings.title: CPI binary at 13:30 GMT
risk_warnings.body: Primary risk event of the week. Pre-position carefully: we are 2/3 size on short EUR/USD going in. Soft surprise (≤+0.2% core) is the asymmetric tail — dollar gives back the week's gains in an hour.

risk_warnings.title: USD/JPY 153.00 intervention zone
risk_warnings.body: Hot CPI lifts spot through 153.00 cleanly. MoF watching; verbal intervention risk before action. Manage long USD/JPY topside vol with awareness of the level.

risk_warnings.title: Treasury 10y auction tail risk
risk_warnings.body: Indirect bid has been weak since March. A tail >+1bp re-prices the long end 2-3bp and pressures the 5s30s steepener we hold.

consensus_calls.impact: September cut conviction the swing variable

geopolitical.narrative: Geopolitical risk premium has compressed across the major axes over the past week. US-China trade dialogue remains constructive at the margin; Middle East tail risk has been priced out of front-month Brent (no headline premium currently). The G7 communiqué released earlier this week reaffirmed coordination on tariff harmonisation — a modest USD tailwind.

regions.name: US – China
regions.headline: Trade dialogue constructive; tariff framework stable
regions.detail: Three rounds of working-level talks in April produced no breakdown. Treasury Sec Yellen's Bretton Woods remarks today the next signal.

regions.name: Russia – Ukraine
regions.headline: Frontline static; secondary sanctions enforcement up
regions.detail: EU 14th sanctions package adopted last week; commodity flow disruptions priced into 1m Brent calendars.

regions.name: Middle East
regions.headline: No new flashpoints; tanker traffic normalised
regions.detail: Strait of Hormuz transit volumes at 6m highs. Brent geo premium ~$2/bbl, down from $5 in March.

regions.headline: Routine PLA patrols; no new escalation
regions.detail: No incremental signal. Watch is on the upcoming presidential cabinet confirmations.
